message:
feat(tui/components): add shared HelpOverlay and KeyHint patterns (ntm-952.1.2)

Implement reusable help overlay and key hint components for consistent
keyboard shortcut documentation across all TUI surfaces. This establishes
the design system foundation for discoverable keybindings.

New help.go component:
- KeyHint struct: represents a single keybinding (key + description)
- RenderKeyHint(): styled key hint with background badge + description
- RenderKeyHintCompact(): minimal styling for narrow terminals
- RenderHelpBar(): width-aware horizontal bar of key hints with
  progressive truncation (rightmost hints dropped first)
- HelpOverlay(): modal-style overlay with grouped keybinding sections

HelpBar features:
- Width-tier aware: compact mode at TierNarrow
- Progressive truncation: removes hints from right until fits
- Custom separator support
- Respects theme colors (Surface0, Text, Overlay)

HelpOverlay features:
- Grouped sections with titles (Navigation, Actions, General)
- Right-aligned keys for visual consistency
- Auto-sizing with optional max width cap
- Footer hint: "Press ? or Esc to close"
- Uses Help icon from icons package

Preset hint providers:
- DefaultPaletteHints(): navigate, quick select, select, back
- DefaultDashboardHints(): navigate, select, zoom, context, mail, refresh, quit
- PaletteHelpSections(): Navigation, Actions, General groups
- DashboardHelpSections(): Navigation, Pane Actions, View Controls, General

Icons package update:
- Added Help field to IconSet struct
- NerdFonts: "" (keyboard icon)
- Unicode/ASCII: "?" fallback

Test coverage:
- TestRenderKeyHint, TestRenderKeyHintCompact
- TestRenderHelpBar: multiple hints, empty, truncation, custom separator
- TestHelpOverlay: sections, default title, footer hint
- TestDefaultHints, TestHelpSections: preset validation

Acceptance criteria met:
- Shared help affordance available for palette + dashboard integration
- Key hints are width-aware via TierForWidth and progressive truncation
- Escape/back behavior documented in help sections

Part of UX Design System sub-epic (ntm-952.1). Closes ntm-952.1.2.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-952","title":"EPIC: Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)","description":"# Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)

## Goal
Make NTM feel calm, obvious, and premium — like a best-in-class product — while staying true to terminal constraints.

This is a **polish program epic**: it coordinates UX improvements across CLI + TUI, emphasizing:
- Clear information hierarchy (less visual noise)
- Predictable navigation (stable keymaps + help overlays)
- Actionable copy (every error tells you what to do next)
- Smoothness (minimal flicker/jitter, fast refresh, responsive interactions)
- Accessibility (reduced motion, icon fallbacks, contrast)

## Target User Journeys
1. **First run**: install → `ntm deps` → `ntm tutorial` → `ntm spawn` → `ntm palette` → `ntm dashboard`
2. **Daily loop**: attach → palette → send → dashboard → copy/save → detach
3. **Remote over SSH**: sessions persist, minimal surprises, no brittle quoting, clear "where am I" context

## Scope
- CLI: copywriting, error remediation, progress affordances
- Palette: search + target selection clarity + recents/favorites
- Dashboard: stable layout + help/legend + better empty/loading/error states
- Theme/accessibility: reduced motion + safe icon fallbacks + contrast
- Performance: refresh budgets, caching, graceful degradation
- Docs alignment (update existing docs; avoid new doc sprawl)

## Out of Scope (tracked elsewhere)
- New major product surfaces (e.g., VS Code extension) → see `ntm-97c5`
- New data sources/panels already covered under dashboard program → see `ntm-jpp1`
- Large config discovery work → see `ntm-bke.1`

## Related / Dependencies (foundations)
- Dashboard foundation: `ntm-jpp1`, `ntm-im5l`, `ntm-p99w`, `ntm-amou`
- Width + layout primitives: `ntm-ci35`
- Refresh cadence: `ntm-0c8q`
- Theme system: `ntm-ed5`, `ntm-ube`
- Context/session inference: `ntm-4ji`, `ntm-bke.1`","design":"## Design Principles (Stripe-level in terminal)
- **Clarity over cleverness**: effects exist to support hierarchy, not compete with content.
- **Predictability**: stable keymaps; no surprise mode switches; consistent back/escape behavior.
- **Actionability**: errors include cause + fix + suggested next command.
- **Continuity**: preserve scroll position / selection where possible; avoid jitter.
- **Graceful fallback**: icons, colors, widths, and animations degrade cleanly.
---

## How to Use This Epic

This epic is intentionally structured to keep UX work **actionable** and **non-duplicative**:
- Foundations already tracked elsewhere (dashboard, theme, config discovery).
- This epic focuses on the “last mile” that makes the product feel premium.

### Recommended Order
1. **Shared UX primitives** (`ntm-952.1.*`) → makes later work consistent.
2. **CLI remediation + session inference** (`ntm-952.2.*`) → improves day-to-day flows immediately.
3. **Palette + dashboard overlays + stability** (`ntm-952.3.*`, `ntm-952.4.*`) → the premium feel.
4. **A11y + perf** (`ntm-952.5.*`, `ntm-952.6.*`) → smooth + safe defaults.
5. **Docs alignment** (`ntm-952.7.*`) at the end of each sub-track.

### Success Heuristics
- If something fails, users know exactly what to do next.
- Key bindings are discoverable without reading docs.
- The UI is stable under refresh: selection doesn’t jump, panes don’t “wiggle”.
- Reduced motion + icon fallbacks make the UI usable everywhere.","acceptance_criteria":"- A consistent help/legend affordance exists in palette + dashboard.
- CLI errors are standardized and always include a remediation suggestion.
- Reduced-motion mode exists and disables pulsing/shimmer animations.
- Dashboard/palette interactions feel stable (minimal flicker/jump under refresh).
- Docs reflect the actual UX (keys, tiers, environment variables).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.798667661-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-jpp1","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ed5","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-bke.1","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ci35","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-0c8q","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ji","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ube","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-952.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: UX Design System & Consistency","description":"Focus: consistency and “calm premium” feel across all TUIs.

Deliverables:
- Shared layout primitives and style tokens (spacing, borders, typography)
- Shared interaction patterns (help overlay, key hints, empty/loading states)
- A small set of conventions used everywhere (no per-screen one-offs)

Non-goals:
- Rewriting all screens at once
- Adding new major product features","design":"## Why this matters
A “Stripe-level” feel in terminal comes from **consistency** more than effects. If every screen invents its own spacing, borders, and help hints, the product feels noisy and amateur.

## Guidance
- Prefer shared helpers over one-off lipgloss styles.
- Design for **width tiers** and **icon sets** from day 1.
- Keep overlays minimal: show the top 10% of keys that drive 90% of usage.

## Downstream impact
Palette/dashboard polish tasks should reuse these primitives rather than re-implementing per-screen versions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.830598777-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

 {"id":"ntm-952.1.3","title":"TUI: Style tokens for spacing/borders/typography","description":"Centralize spacing, border, and text-style conventions into a small set of tokens/helpers to avoid per-screen drift and misalignment.","acceptance_criteria":"- Core style helpers exist and are used in new code\n- Common gutter widths are consistent across screens\n- No new one-off lipgloss styles without a clear reason","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.996387915-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.996387915-06:00","labels":["ux","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-952.1.4","title":"TUI: Regression snapshots for overlays and empty states","description":"Add targeted snapshot/layout tests for the shared overlays and empty/loading/error states to prevent subtle UX regressions across width tiers.","acceptance_criteria":"- Tests cover narrow + split + wide tiers\n- Snapshots include help overlay + empty states\n- CI catches accidental layout drift","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:08.046387947-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:08.046387947-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-952.2","title":"SUB-EPIC: CLI Copy & Error Remediation","description":"Focus: CLI output that’s clear, actionable, and consistent.

Deliverables:
- Standard error format (cause → fix → next command)
- Better session selection/resolution when args omitted
- Consistent “what next” guidance after major commands","design":"## Error copy contract
Every error should include:
1) **What failed** (command + target)
2) **Why** (root cause in plain language)
3) **What to do next** (one concrete command)

## Session resolution contract
When a session argument is missing:
- Prefer deterministic inference (cwd → project/session mapping).
- If ambiguous, show an interactive chooser.
- Always echo the chosen session explicitly.

## Avoid
- One-off flags per command.
- Huge stack traces by default.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.862410571-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,349 @@
+// Package components provides shared TUI building blocks.
+package components
+
+import (
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/icons"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/layout"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/theme"
+)
+
+// KeyHint represents a single keybinding hint (e.g., "↑/↓" → "navigate").
+type KeyHint struct {
+	Key  string // The key(s) to press, e.g., "↑/↓", "Enter", "q"
+	Desc string // Brief description, e.g., "navigate", "select", "quit"
+}
+
+// HelpBarOptions configures HelpBar rendering.
+type HelpBarOptions struct {
+	Hints     []KeyHint // Key hints to display
+	Width     int       // Available width (0 = unlimited)
+	Separator string    // Separator between hints (default: "  ")
+}
+
+// RenderKeyHint renders a single key hint with consistent styling.
+// Returns styled string: "[key] desc"
+func RenderKeyHint(hint KeyHint) string {
+	t := theme.Current()
+
+	keyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Background(t.Surface0).
+		Foreground(t.Text).
+		Bold(true).
+		Padding(0, 1)
+
+	descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Overlay)
+
+	return keyStyle.Render(hint.Key) + " " + descStyle.Render(hint.Desc)
+}
+
+// RenderKeyHintCompact renders a minimal key hint without background.
+// Returns styled string: "key desc" (for narrow widths).
+func RenderKeyHintCompact(hint KeyHint) string {
+	t := theme.Current()
+
+	keyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Text).
+		Bold(true)
+
+	descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Overlay)
+
+	return keyStyle.Render(hint.Key) + " " + descStyle.Render(hint.Desc)
+}
+
+// RenderHelpBar renders a horizontal bar of key hints, respecting width constraints.
+// Hints are progressively hidden from right-to-left if they don't fit.
+// Note: This complements the existing HelpBar struct type in preview.go with a
+// function-based API that supports width-aware truncation.
+func RenderHelpBar(opts HelpBarOptions) string {
+	if len(opts.Hints) == 0 {
+		return ""
+	}
+
+	sep := opts.Separator
+	if sep == "" {
+		sep = "  "
+	}
+
+	// Determine rendering style based on width tier
+	tier := layout.TierForWidth(opts.Width)
+	compact := tier == layout.TierNarrow
+
+	// Build all rendered hints
+	var rendered []string
+	for _, h := range opts.Hints {
+		if compact {
+			rendered = append(rendered, RenderKeyHintCompact(h))
+		} else {
+			rendered = append(rendered, RenderKeyHint(h))
+		}
+	}
+
+	// If no width constraint, return all hints
+	if opts.Width <= 0 {
+		return strings.Join(rendered, sep)
+	}
+
+	// Progressive truncation: remove hints from right until it fits
+	sepWidth := lipgloss.Width(sep)
+	for len(rendered) > 0 {
+		total := 0
+		for i, r := range rendered {
+			total += lipgloss.Width(r)
+			if i > 0 {
+				total += sepWidth
+			}
+		}
+		if total <= opts.Width {
+			break
+		}
+		// Remove rightmost hint
+		rendered = rendered[:len(rendered)-1]
+	}
+
+	return strings.Join(rendered, sep)
+}
+
+// HelpOverlayOptions configures the help overlay appearance.
+type HelpOverlayOptions struct {
+	Title    string       // Overlay title (default: "Keyboard Shortcuts")
+	Sections []HelpSection // Grouped key hints
+	Width    int          // Overlay width (0 = auto-size)
+	MaxWidth int          // Maximum width cap
+}
+
+// HelpSection groups related key hints under a heading.
+type HelpSection struct {
+	Title string    // Section heading, e.g., "Navigation", "Actions"
+	Hints []KeyHint // Key hints in this section
+}
+
+// HelpOverlay renders a modal-style help overlay with grouped keybindings.
+// Designed to be shown when user presses '?' or F1.
+func HelpOverlay(opts HelpOverlayOptions) string {
+	t := theme.Current()
+	ic := icons.Current()
+
+	// Calculate content to determine width
+	var lines []string
+
+	// Title
+	title := opts.Title
+	if title == "" {
+		title = "Keyboard Shortcuts"
+	}
+
+	titleIcon := strings.TrimSpace(ic.Help)
+	if titleIcon == "" {
+		titleIcon = "?"
+	}
+
+	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Primary).
+		Bold(true)
+
+	lines = append(lines, titleStyle.Render(titleIcon+"  "+title))
+	lines = append(lines, "")
+
+	// Styles for sections
+	sectionStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Mauve).
+		Bold(true)
+
+	keyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Text).
+		Bold(true)
+
+	descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Subtext)
+
+	// Find max key width for alignment
+	maxKeyWidth := 0
+	for _, section := range opts.Sections {
+		for _, hint := range section.Hints {
+			w := lipgloss.Width(hint.Key)
+			if w > maxKeyWidth {
+				maxKeyWidth = w
+			}
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Render sections
+	for i, section := range opts.Sections {
+		if section.Title != "" {
+			lines = append(lines, sectionStyle.Render(section.Title))
+		}
+
+		for _, hint := range section.Hints {
+			// Right-align key, left-align description
+			keyPadded := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+				Width(maxKeyWidth).
+				Align(lipgloss.Right).
+				Render(hint.Key)
+
+			line := "  " + keyStyle.Render(keyPadded) + "  " + descStyle.Render(hint.Desc)
+			lines = append(lines, line)
+		}
+
+		// Add spacing between sections (except last)
+		if i < len(opts.Sections)-1 {
+			lines = append(lines, "")
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Add footer hint
+	lines = append(lines, "")
+	footerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Foreground(t.Overlay).
+		Italic(true)
+	lines = append(lines, footerStyle.Render("Press ? or Esc to close"))
+
+	content := strings.Join(lines, "\n")
+
+	// Calculate box width
+	width := opts.Width
+	if width <= 0 {
+		// Auto-size based on content
+		for _, line := range lines {
+			w := lipgloss.Width(line)
+			if w > width {
+				width = w
+			}
+		}
+		width += 4 // Padding
+	}
+
+	if opts.MaxWidth > 0 && width > opts.MaxWidth {
+		width = opts.MaxWidth
+	}
+
+	// Render in a styled box
+	boxStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
+		BorderForeground(t.Blue).
+		Padding(1, 2).
+		Width(width)
+
+	return boxStyle.Render(content)
+}
+
+// CommonNavigationHints returns standard navigation key hints.
+func CommonNavigationHints() []KeyHint {
+	return []KeyHint{
+		{Key: "↑/↓", Desc: "navigate"},
+		{Key: "j/k", Desc: "navigate"},
+	}
+}
+
+// CommonSelectionHints returns standard selection key hints.
+func CommonSelectionHints() []KeyHint {
+	return []KeyHint{
+		{Key: "Enter", Desc: "select"},
+		{Key: "1-9", Desc: "quick select"},
+	}
+}
+
+// CommonQuitHints returns standard quit/back key hints.
+func CommonQuitHints() []KeyHint {
+	return []KeyHint{
+		{Key: "Esc", Desc: "back"},
+		{Key: "q", Desc: "quit"},
+	}
+}
+
+// DefaultPaletteHints returns the standard palette key hints.
+func DefaultPaletteHints() []KeyHint {
+	return []KeyHint{
+		{Key: "↑/↓", Desc: "navigate"},
+		{Key: "1-9", Desc: "quick select"},
+		{Key: "Enter", Desc: "select"},
+		{Key: "Esc", Desc: "back"},
+	}
+}
+
+// DefaultDashboardHints returns the standard dashboard key hints.
+func DefaultDashboardHints() []KeyHint {
+	return []KeyHint{
+		{Key: "↑↓", Desc: "navigate"},
+		{Key: "1-9", Desc: "select"},
+		{Key: "z", Desc: "zoom"},
+		{Key: "c", Desc: "context"},
+		{Key: "m", Desc: "mail"},
+		{Key: "r", Desc: "refresh"},
+		{Key: "q", Desc: "quit"},
+	}
+}
+
+// PaletteHelpSections returns help sections for the palette overlay.
+func PaletteHelpSections() []HelpSection {
+	return []HelpSection{
+		{
+			Title: "Navigation",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "↑ / k", Desc: "Move up"},
+				{Key: "↓ / j", Desc: "Move down"},
+				{Key: "1-9", Desc: "Quick select item"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			Title: "Actions",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "Enter", Desc: "Select command"},
+				{Key: "1-4", Desc: "Select target (in target phase)"},
+				{Key: "Type", Desc: "Filter commands"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			Title: "General",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "Esc", Desc: "Go back / Cancel"},
+				{Key: "q", Desc: "Quit palette"},
+				{Key: "Ctrl+C", Desc: "Force quit"},
+			},
+		},
+	}
+}
+
+// DashboardHelpSections returns help sections for the dashboard overlay.
+func DashboardHelpSections() []HelpSection {
+	return []HelpSection{
+		{
+			Title: "Navigation",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "↑ / k", Desc: "Move up"},
+				{Key: "↓ / j", Desc: "Move down"},
+				{Key: "← / h", Desc: "Previous panel"},
+				{Key: "→ / l", Desc: "Next panel"},
+				{Key: "Tab", Desc: "Cycle panels"},
+				{Key: "1-9", Desc: "Quick select pane"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			Title: "Pane Actions",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "z / Enter", Desc: "Zoom to pane"},
+				{Key: "c", Desc: "Show context"},
+				{Key: "m", Desc: "Agent mail"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			Title: "View Controls",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "r", Desc: "Refresh data"},
+				{Key: "?", Desc: "Toggle help"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			Title: "General",
+			Hints: []KeyHint{
+				{Key: "q / Esc", Desc: "Quit dashboard"},
+				{Key: "Ctrl+C", Desc: "Force quit"},
+			},
+		},
+	}
+}
